schema_notes: Per-billionaire record matching larry_ellison.v3.json schema. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events canonically scoped by event_role. Classification for Tier A/B/C happens downstream.
generated_by: scrooge v3 single-subject deep research — Stephen M. Ross

person.wealth_source: Related Companies (real estate development — Hudson Yards, Deutsche Bank Center/Time Warner Center); Miami Dolphins NFL team (~94% stake); Equinox Holdings (SoulCycle, Equinox Fitness); Related Ross (West Palm Beach); Formula 1 Miami Grand Prix stake
person.years_as_billionaire_note: Ross first appeared on Forbes 400 around 2008; has been billionaire continuously since.

net_worth.range_note: Caproasia (Jan 2026) cites $17B; ForbesRichest tracker $17.0B; Wikipedia 2020 snapshot $10.1B (dated). March 2026 Dolphins 1% sale to Lin Bin at $125M implies $12.5B valuation for Dolphins holding co alone — consistent with $15-17B total personal fortune. Dec 2024 Ross sold 13% of Dolphins/assets at $8.1B valuation.

sources.value_note: March 2026 1% sale at $125M implies Dolphins holding co ~$12.5B

net_worth.liquidity_notes: Heavily illiquid. Related Companies is private. Miami Dolphins (~94%) is a single sports-franchise asset. Equinox/SoulCycle is private. Real estate portfolio ($60B+ developed) is asset-heavy but encumbered by debt and partners. 25% liquidity is reasonable for giving-capacity-now; recent partial-stake sales (Dec 2024 13% Dolphins at $8.1B val; March 2026 1% at $12.5B val) show ability to monetize selectively.

cited_events.recipient: Various (recipients not disclosed in ProPublica summary)
cited_events.notes: Sharp spike vs surrounding years. Foundation assets only ~$11.7M at end of 2024 — implies funding passed through quickly (possible DAF transfer or large one-time pledge payment). Grant recipients not disclosed in ProPublica summary; 990-PF Schedule B would resolve.

cited_events.recipient: University of Michigan Stephen M. Ross School of Business
cited_events.notes: Naming gift for business school; at the time one of the largest B-school gifts ever.

cited_events.recipient: University of Michigan — $100M Ross School of Business + $100M U-M Athletics
cited_events.notes: Largest single gift in U-M history at the time. Athletics campus renamed Stephen M. Ross Athletic Campus. Widely cited as the largest athletics gift ever to a U.S. university at the time of announcement.

cited_events.recipient: World Resources Institute — WRI Ross Center for Sustainable Cities
Ross Initiative in Sports for Equality (RISE) — 501(c)(3) founding
cited_events.notes: Founding commitment cited as $30M total since 2015. Split: estimated $17M pre-2020 founding + base, $13M 2020 additional (see ross_rise_2020_13m). Total commitment $30M as of June 2020 statement. If commitments not yet fully disbursed, treat as pledge vs grant distinction.

cited_events.recipient: University of Michigan Ross School of Business — Student Success & faculty
cited_events.notes: University of Michigan press release confirms lifetime giving reaches $378M with this gift.

cited_events.recipient: University of Michigan — Detroit Center for Innovation (DCI)
cited_events.notes: Launch gift for 190,000 sq ft research/education center on former Wayne County Jail site, partnered with Dan Gilbert's land contribution. Multi-year pledge.

cited_events.recipient: World Resources Institute — Ross Center for Sustainable Cities
cited_events.notes: Brings cumulative WRI Ross Center commitment to $100M (with 2014 $30.5M plus prior smaller).
cited_events.dedup_note: If any portion was paid through SMRF foundation (EIN 387104084), 2020 SMRF grant_out $3.0M would need to be checked against this; unlikely given WRI's $63.5M far exceeds SMRF's 2020 disbursement.

cited_events.recipient: Ross Initiative in Sports for Equality (RISE)
cited_events.notes: Four-year commitment announced post-George Floyd. Brings total RISE commitments to $30M since 2015 founding.

pledges_and_announcements.pledged_pct_wealth_note: No specific percentage stated in letter — commits to 'substantial majority'
pledges_and_announcements.status_source: IPS Giving Pledge at 15 (July 2025): only 9 of 256 living/deceased pledgers have fulfilled; Ross not among the 9 fulfillers cited.
pledges_and_announcements.pledge_letter_quote: I commit to give away the substantial majority of my assets to charitable causes and give back to the society of which my success was derived.
pledges_and_announcements.notes: Signed 2013. Net worth at signing estimated ~$3-4B (Forbes 2013). 'Substantial majority' = conservatively 50%+ = ~$1.5-2B at 2013. Current net worth $17B; 50% target = ~$8.5B. Documented observable giving lifetime ~$665M (see rollup). Gap ~$7.8B against 50% floor.
pledges_and_announcements.focus_areas: Education, arts, healthcare, sustainable urban development

pledges_and_announcements.recipient: Vanderbilt University — West Palm Beach graduate campus
pledges_and_announcements.notes: Reported as $50M toward Vanderbilt's $520M West Palm graduate campus plan. Disbursement schedule not verified.

political_giving.substitution_note: Not counted as philanthropy. NBER W26616 estimates political giving displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33 per $1.
political_giving.observed_total_note: Minimum floor from press sources only; FEC direct pull not yet run. Ross is a major Republican/Trump-era donor but primarily personal-check-size (hundreds of thousands) rather than Ellison-scale super-PAC megagifts. Common Sense NYC super-PAC seeded with $1M personal + $500K via Vote for NYC's Future (2021). Historic OpenSecrets coverage (2019) cited Ross as top-200 donor at ~$452K lifetime, 93% Republican.

sources.event_description: Personal funding of Common Sense NYC super-PAC, 2021 NYC Council primary
Aug 2019 Hamptons Trump fundraiser — raised $12M total across two events; up to $250K/plate. Ross hosted at his Southampton home. Triggered Equinox/SoulCycle boycott calls.

political_giving.todo: FEC bulk pull against Ross name/address + Related Companies employer to tighten number. Common Sense NYC + Voters of NYC combined raised $2M+ in 2021 alone; Ross may have given more across all cycles than the $3M floor.

foundations_active.name: Stephen M Ross Foundation
foundations_active.trustee: Jordan Park Trust Company LLC / Perspecta Trust LLC
foundations_active.officers_compensation: ZERO_COMP (all officers/trustees report $0 compensation; Ross serves on Distribution and Investment Committees)
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: Extreme year-over-year volatility: $38.9M (2023) vs $40K (2022) vs $11M (2019). Foundation operates as flow-through; Ross makes most philanthropy directly rather than through this vehicle. 2024 $396K / $11.7M assets = 3.4% payout — below 5% IRS minimum for private foundations. Flag: LOW_PAYOUT_2024.
foundations_active.red_flag_daf_transfers: NOT_YET_VERIFIED — 2023 spike of $38.9M against $11.7M year-end assets raises a DAF-transfer hypothesis but ProPublica summary page does not disclose recipients. Schedule B/Part XV review needed.

foundations_active.name: Related Ross Foundation Inc
foundations_active.notes: Separate entity tied to Related Ross (West Palm Beach Related Companies subsidiary). Financials not yet pulled. TODO.

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No DAF accounts publicly identified in Ross's name. 2023 SMRF $38.9M grant spike is a flag worth investigating — SMRF may have routed through a sponsoring DAF or passed through to RISE/WRI/DCI. Until Schedule B is examined, treat as unknown lower bound.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: No CZI-style philanthropic LLC. Related Companies is for-profit; Related Ross is for-profit real estate.

trusts_disclosed_existence_structure_unk.source_quote: Jordan Park Trust Company LLC is the trustee of SMRF; Perspecta Trust LLC administers. Typical structure for UHNW estate planning.
trusts_disclosed_existence_structure_unk.classification_note: Foundation is trust-held but specific grantor/family trust structures for Ross personal assets are not publicly disclosed. Ross has transferred value through Dolphins holding co stake sales (Dec 2024 13% at $8.1B val; March 2026 1% at $12.5B val); underlying ownership structure not public.

red_flags.evidence: Signed 2013 Giving Pledge committing 'substantial majority' of assets. Net worth has grown from ~$3-4B (2013) to $17B (2026). Observable giving ~$665M lifetime — far below 50% floor (~$8.5B at current NW). IPS 2025 report lists only 9 of 256 pledgers fulfilled; Ross not among them.

2019 Hamptons Trump fundraiser host (up to $250K/plate); funded Common Sense NYC super-PAC with $1M+ personal; $500K to Vote for NYC's Future; historic 93% Republican giving pattern (OpenSecrets). Triggered consumer boycotts of Equinox/SoulCycle.
SMRF 2024 grants $396,414 vs end-of-year assets $11.7M = 3.4% payout, below 5% IRS minimum for private foundations. Single-year anomaly following massive 2023 distribution; check 2025 filing.

2023 SMRF grants_out $38.9M vs foundation assets $11.7M implies flow-through with no visible recipient list in ProPublica summary. Could be direct gift (e.g., to UMich, WRI, RISE) or DAF transfer. Until Schedule B examined, flag as unverified.

Miami Dolphins (~94%) + Related Companies private equity are extremely illiquid. Unlike public-market billionaires, Ross has strong structural reason to not liquidate for giving; he generates giving capacity only through stake sales (Dec 2024, March 2026) or dividends.

red_flags_not_present: NO_OBSERVABLE_GIVING (clear multi-hundred-million track record to UMich, WRI, RISE, DCI)

rollup.observable_giving_method: Lifetime direct gifts: UMich 2004 $100M + UMich 2013 $200M + WRI 2014 $30.5M + UMich 2017 $50M + UMich 2020 DCI $100M + WRI 2020 $63.5M + RISE $30M + Vanderbilt $50M (pledged) = $624M direct gifts. Plus SMRF 990-PF grants_out 2019-2024 sum = $53.2M. Total $677.2M. Subtract ~$12M overlap hypothesis (SMRF funds that may have passed through to UMich/WRI) = $665.7M. Does NOT count political giving.
rollup.observable_giving_alternate_note: University of Michigan's own press releases characterize lifetime UMich giving as $478M (post-2020 figures). UMich-only + WRI ($100M) + RISE ($30M) + Vanderbilt pledge ($50M) = $658M; adding foundation grants-out 2019-2024 ($53M net of double-count) gives similar ~$665M. Two independent paths to same rough total.

rollup.expected_giving_method: 50% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.25 × $17B = $4.25B; tenure cap saturated (18 years). Expected = $2.125B. Uses 50% not 10% because Ross's own pledge commits to 'substantial majority' (≥50%).
rollup.expected_giving_alt_note: Under standard Scrooge 10%-of-liquid norm (not Ross's own 50% pledge): $4.25B × 10% = $425M. Observable $665M EXCEEDS this. Under Ross's own pledged 50% standard, he falls short by ~$1.46B.

hidden_upper_usd.daf_uplift_note: 2023 SMRF $38.9M spike is the main uplift candidate if it routed through a DAF. Anonymous cohort DAF giving upper at $50M.
hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not yet checked. Zero until verified.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: BofA/Lilly 2024: 69% of HNW donors give anonymously at least sometimes. Ross is relatively public about giving (naming gifts); assume anonymous giving is a small fraction. 0.5% × $17B = $85M upper bound (generous given he name-brands most gifts).
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: No publicly disclosed religious giving. Zero.
hidden_upper_usd.trust_outcome_method: Ross has publicly pledged 'substantial majority' and has heir children. Estate could eventually disburse to charity or to family. Upper envelope $2B represents a plausible death-transfer charitable share (roughly 10-15% of current NW) — DOES NOT count toward observable for Tier classification.
hidden_upper_usd.total_method: daf + llc + offshore + anonymous + religious + trust = 50M + 0 + 0 + 85M + 0 + 2B

rollup.tier_rule: Under the standard 10%-of-liquid norm, Ross's observable giving ($665M) EXCEEDS the $425M floor — suggests NOT a Scrooge by that metric. Under his own 2013 pledge commitment ('substantial majority' → 50%+), the $2.125B target is unmet (31% of expected). Tier B reflects this tension: visible mid-hundreds-of-millions of documented giving, but a pledge commitment that implies ~5-10x current lifetime total. Worth watching the 2023 SMRF spike and any post-2024 gift announcements.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Ross is not a classic Scrooge case. He has given $478M lifetime to University of Michigan alone (largest donor in UM history), $100M cumulative to WRI, $30M to RISE, $100M to DCI. The Scrooge lens flags him because: (1) signed Giving Pledge but hasn't approached the 'substantial majority' threshold given his $17B fortune, (2) heavy political-fundraiser role for Trump, (3) wealth is locked in illiquid sports/real estate assets, (4) 2024 foundation payout anomaly.
rollup.expected_5pct_tenure_method: 5% × net_worth_best × liquidity_pct × years_as_billionaire (uncapped tenure); computed by aggregate_v3.py
rollup.observable_reconciliation_note: Published `observable_giving_usd` reflects agent judgment about attributable giving. `observable_from_events_usd` is the strict sum of canonical grant_out + direct_gift event amounts. A gap can mean self-reported lifetime figures beyond cited events (rollup > sum) or exclusion of inherited-foundation / family-foundation portions (rollup < sum).

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: Computed with v2 formula. Lower than Ellison/Zuckerberg scores because observable giving is genuinely substantial. DO NOT PUBLISH this number — publish tiers only per PLAN.md.

summary_150_words: Stephen Ross (b. 1940, Related Companies founder, ~94% owner of Miami Dolphins) is worth ~$17B in 2026. He signed the Giving Pledge in 2013, committing 'substantial majority' of assets to charity. Observable lifetime giving is ~$665M: $478M to University of Michigan alone (largest donor in UM history, including $200M in 2013 split between Ross School of Business and athletics — the biggest athletics gift at the time), $100M cumulative to WRI Ross Center for Sustainable Cities, $30M founding RISE (Ross Initiative in Sports for Equality) in 2015, and $100M for University of Michigan's Detroit Center for Innovation (2020). The Stephen M Ross Foundation (EIN 38-7104084, NH) shows erratic payouts — $38.9M in 2023, then just $396K in 2024 (below 5% IRS minimum). Political: major Trump-era Republican donor, host of the notorious 2019 Hamptons fundraiser ($250K/plate) and primary funder of NYC's anti-progressive Common Sense NYC super-PAC. Tier B — real giving, real pledge gap, real political substitution.
